hero.title: /dev/notion
hero.description: Not just another CRUD API for your SaaS.

cliCallout.text: Start building on the Notion CLI or

keynote.caption: See the Developer Platform in action.

sync.heading: Sync any data source into Notion.
sync.subheading: Continuously upsert external records into a Notion Database with Workers, a declarative schema, and a persistent cursor.
sync.demoAriaLabel: A code example showing a Worker syncing tickets from Zendesk into a Notion database titled Support Tickets.

tools.heading: Build any tool for your agents.
tools.subheading: Write custom tools for Notion Agents that generate assets, query live data, and hit any API.

presentation.ariaLabel: A chat showing a Custom Agent generating a presentation from a Notion doc with a code snippet of the presentation tool.
presentation.codeHtml: worker.tool("createPresentation", { title: "Create Presentation", description: "Reads a Notion page and creates a PowerPoint presentation from its content. Each heading becomes a new slide. The generated .pptx file is uploaded to the bottom of the page.", schema: j.object({ pageId: j.string() }), execute: async ({ pageId }, { notion }) => { // Fetch page content as markdown and parse into slides const pageTitle = await getPageTitle(notion, pageId); const markdown = await getPageMarkdown(pageId); const slides = groupMarkdownIntoSlides(markdown, pageTitle); // Build the .pptx file const filename = `${pageTitle}.pptx`; const buffer = await buildPresentation(pageTitle, slides); // Upload to Notion and append to the page await uploadToNotion(notion, pageId, filename, buffer); return `Created presentation "${pageTitle}" with ${slides.length + 1} slides (1 title + ${slides.length} content) and added it to the page.`; }, });

dataWarehouse.ariaLabel: A chat showing a Custom Agent querying a data warehouse in Notion with a code snippet of the data warehouse query tool.
dataWarehouse.codeHtml: worker.tool("queryRevenue", { title: "Query Revenue", description: "Run a SQL query against the deals warehouse table.", schema: j.object({ query: j .string() .describe("e.g. SELECT SUM(amount) AS revenue WHERE region = 'North America'"), }), execute: async ({ query }) => snowflake.query(query.replace(/(\s+WHERE|$)/i, " FROM deals$1")), });

browser.ariaLabel: A chat showing a Custom Agent browsing the web in Notion and placing an order, with the LLM prompt used to create the browser tool.
browser.codeHtml: <!-- Copy and paste this into your coding agent of choice. --> Build a worker that lets my Notion agent order food from DoorDash using Browserbase. I want three tools: - listFavorites — Returns my saved meals (e.g. "friday night thai" = Pad See Ew + Green Curry from Siam Garden). - orderFavorite — Takes a meal name, shows me what it's about to order and the estimated total, and asks for confirmation before placing it. - checkOrder — Returns delivery ETA and driver status.

toolChat.replyText: All set. Here’s the new Powerpoint for your next pitch. Break a leg!

webhooks.heading: Trigger Notion workflows from anywhere.
webhooks.subheading: Listen for incoming webhooks from any app, then run workflows with Notion Agents, pages, databases, and external APIs.
webhooks.ariaLabel: An animation showing external platform webhooks triggering Workers to perform actions in Notion or externally.

workers.heading: All of this, on a hosted runtime.
workers.subheading: Workers are isolated sandboxes managed by Notion, so the code behind your syncs, tools, and workflows runs on our infra instead of your servers.

terminal.ariaLabel: A terminal window showing a worker being deployed to a Notion-hosted runtime.
terminal.html: $ ntn deploy worker [build] Deploying... [build] Uploading to Notion... [build] Worker updated... Capabilities: tool queryCustomers tool moreOfficeSnacks sync linearTickets

ticker.message: Syncs, agent tools, and webhook triggers are available in beta.

externalAgents.heading: Bring your favorite agents into Notion.
externalAgents.subheading: Use external agents like Claude, Cursor, Decagon, or one you’ve built, in a shared interface for your team and agents.

cards.title: Mention
cards.body: Collaborate with agents like teammates. @mention them in any page, comment, or chat with them directly.

image.alt: A chat within Notion showing a user interacting with external agents to kick off a coding task.

cards.title: Assign
cards.body: Hand off work to your agents from any task, or trigger them in parallel.

image.alt: Notion database with a list of tasks, and one task being assigned to an external coding agent.

cards.title: Orchestrate
cards.body: Watch agents think, call tools, and act across Notion and other apps with your review and approval when it matters.

image.alt: A chat with an external agent in Notion, showing its thought process, reading documents, and calling tools.

cards.title: BYO Agent
cards.body: Bring your in‑house agents into Notion with the External Agents API. Notion gives them triggers, tools, and permissions so they show up as first‑class collaborators on all the same surfaces.

terminal.ariaLabel: A list of API endpoints that a third-party agent can call to have it behave like a Custom Agent in Notion.

cli.title: Notion CLI
cli.body: Let coding agents use token‑efficient commands to build and deploy syncs and tools to our Workers runtime.

api.title: Notion API
api.body: Turn docs into Markdown and build internal tools with PATs or shared connections.

mcp.title: Notion MCP
mcp.body: Search, read, and edit pages and databases with up to 91% fewer tokens.
mcp.command: claude mcp add --transport http notion https://mcp.notion.com/mcp

sdk.title: Notion Agent SDK
sdk.body: Bring your Agents into any app. Trigger runs via API, keep multi-turn threads, and stream responses in real time.
sdk.codeAriaLabel: A code snippet showing how to use the Agent SDK to create a new agent.
sdk.codeHtml: import { NotionAgentsClient } from "@notionhq/agents-client"; const client = new NotionAgentsClient(); const agent = await client.agents.agent(id); await agent.chat({ message: "Hello, World!" });

endcap.heading: Any data. Any tool. Any agent. All in Notion.
endcap.diagramAlt: Diagram of Notion’s developer platform connecting external agents, data sources, and triggers to Notion context and custom agents through CLI, MCP, API, SDK, and workers.
